title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Hercule Poirot and Carter debate the true meaning of the message left by Miss Jean (also known as Jane or Jenny). Poirot argues that Jean’s precise use of grammar suggests her instruction "not to let them talk" was not a request to protect a killer, but a specific warning to prevent the police from opening the victims' mouths to discover the gold cufflinks engraved with the initials "PIJ." Poirot believes Jean anticipated she would be the next target and that the initials belong to an important figure. He posits that because cufflinks are typically sold in pairs, a fourth cufflink likely exists and was intended for Jean's mouth. While Carter initially dismisses this as ridiculous, he eventually acknowledges the possibility of further victims.

Poirot insists on visiting the Bluxton Hotel the following morning to investigate the murders, planning to meet Carter there after searching for Jean. The next morning, Carter waits at the Browns Hotel, feeling uneasy about his involvement. While Carter is speaking with the hotel manager, Luca Lazari, Poirot arrives looking downcast. He informs Carter that he was unable to find Jean, as she failed to meet him at the café as they had planned.
